title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Poirot engages in a psychological discussion with Lynn Marchmont, who claims she has changed following her service in the Women's Royal Naval Service and her time living abroad. Poirot disputes this, suggesting she has always been a person who seeks to escape her current circumstances. Lynn admits she has struggled with her feelings for David Hunter; while she is drawn to him, she admits she has never fully trusted him due to rumors that he was merely Rosaline's boyfriend. She notes that while David's commanding officer praised his bravery, she remains uncertain if he is responsible for the murder.

Lynn reveals that on the night of the murder, she received a call from her Aunt Kitty, who used a public phone booth because her own phone was broken. She also received a call from David, who stated he was leaving her life because he felt he was no good for her. Lynn expresses a collective sense of ill will toward Rosaline, noting that the family feels a desire for her death, though she attributes this to the harm Rosaline has caused through jealousy and resentment. Lynn describes Rosaline as looking like a ghost and being terrified at Frognack, where she has refused help from family members, including Aunt Frances, Aunt Cassie, and Lynn herself. Lynn mentions that Rosaline seems to listen to David and prefers receiving supplies from Rolly Claude.

Poirot decides they must visit Frognack. Upon arriving at the estate established by Gordon Claude, they are met by a maid. While waiting for the maid to deliver a message, Poirot observes that the living room at Frognack is filled with high-quality, tasteful items, yet lacks any personal touch or evidence of Rosaline's individual personality.
